video: Video 3: Create an Asset Library with Megascans (11:10)

node_sequence.description: Place assets from gallery into the scene
node_sequence.notes: Drag assets from Asset Gallery into viewport. Layout LOP modes: place, line, paint, stack, scale. CRITICAL: Change from 'Point Instancer' to 'Instanceable Reference' — required for physics to work.

node_sequence.description: Add and run physics simulation on placed assets
node_sequence.notes: Select assets -> Add Physics -> Use Physics. Enables real-time physics interactions (collisions, gravity). Select individual or all assets. Transform: T, Rotate: R, Scale: E.

node_sequence.description: Grid acts as static collision surface for physics
node_sequence.notes: The viewport grid serves as the static collision ground plane. No special setup needed — it's implicit.

constraints: CRITICAL: Layout LOP must use 'Instanceable Reference' mode, NOT 'Point Instancer'. Point Instancer will not work with physics.
Physics workflow: select assets in Edit LOP -> Add Physics -> Use Physics.
The grid acts as a static collision surface — no special setup needed.
Real-time physics interactions include collisions and gravity.
Transform hotkeys during physics: T (translate), R (rotate), E (scale).
Layout LOP modes: place (single), line (along line), paint (brush), stack (vertical), scale (resize).
test_criteria: Layout workflow: (1) Layout LOP in 'Instanceable Reference' mode places assets from gallery. (2) Edit LOP with Add Physics + Use Physics enables collisions. (3) Assets fall under gravity and collide with grid. (4) Verify Point Instancer mode does NOT work with physics (negative test). (5) Transform tools (T/R/E) work on physics-enabled assets.

quick_render_chain.description: After layout and physics, the quick render chain is: Layout -> Edit (physics) -> Karma Physical Sky -> Karma Setup -> Camera. Camera shortcut: click camera shift tool icon to create camera at current viewport angle.

layout_modes.place: Single asset placement at click location

physics_notes.reason: Point Instancer creates lightweight instances that don't carry individual physics properties. Instanceable Reference creates proper USD references that each support physics attributes.
physics_notes.verification_note: The exact technical reason why Point Instancer doesn't work with physics needs live Houdini verification. The source states it as a rule but doesn't explain the underlying USD mechanism.
